title: Meridian EU Infrastructure Debt Fund
content:
MERIDIAN WEALTH PARTNERS - EU INFRASTRUCTURE DEBT FUND FACTSHEET

Fund Overview: The Meridian EU Infrastructure Debt Fund (ISIN: LU2458291847) is a specialized alternative investment fund targeting senior secured debt investments in European infrastructure assets. Launched in Q2 2023, the fund has demonstrated consistent performance with a net IRR of 7.2% and currently manages €487 million in assets under management.

Investment Strategy: Our investment committee, led by Portfolio Manager Robert J. Williams, focuses on essential infrastructure sectors including renewable energy, transportation networks, and digital infrastructure across core EU markets. The fund maintains a conservative approach with 75% allocation to investment-grade credits and an average loan-to-value ratio of 65%. Target investments range from €15-50 million with typical tenors of 7-12 years.

Regulatory Framework: This fund operates under AIFMD regulations as a qualifying professional investor fund. All client suitability assessments must comply with MiFID II Article 25 requirements, ensuring appropriate risk profiling and investment knowledge verification. The fund is authorized by the Luxembourg CSSF and passport throughout the EU under AIFMD Article 32.

Risk Profile: Classified as moderate risk, the fund exhibits lower volatility compared to equity alternatives while maintaining attractive yield potential. Key risks include interest rate sensitivity, construction completion risk for greenfield projects, and regulatory changes affecting infrastructure sectors. The fund employs comprehensive hedging strategies with 60% of currency exposure hedged to EUR.

Performance Metrics: As of March 2025, the fund has delivered quarterly distributions averaging 1.8% with zero defaults in the current portfolio. The weighted average credit rating stands at BBB+ with geographic diversification across Germany (28%), France (22%), Netherlands (18%), and other EU markets (32%). Duration risk is managed at 4.2 years average.

Client Suitability: This product requires Tier 3 authorization and minimum investment of €250,000. Suitable for sophisticated investors seeking portfolio diversification and steady income generation. Account #4821-7734 represents our typical institutional allocation model for pension funds and insurance companies.

Subscription Process: New investors must complete enhanced due diligence including source of funds verification and appropriateness testing under MiFID II guidelines. Subscription documents are processed monthly with T+5 settlement. For detailed terms and private placement memorandum, contact our client services team at (212) 555-0147.

Tax Considerations: The fund structure provides tax-efficient distributions through Luxembourg's extensive treaty network. EU investors may benefit from reduced withholding tax rates, though individual tax advice is recommended. FATCA and CRS reporting obligations apply to all non-EU beneficial owners.

Liquidity Terms: The fund offers quarterly redemption windows with 90-day notice period. Redemption proceeds are typically settled within 30 business days, subject to portfolio liquidity and market conditions. Early redemption may incur fees of up to 2% of redeemed amount.

This document is confidential and intended solely for qualified professional clients. Past performance does not guarantee future results. All figures are unaudited and subject to change.
